summary: Iteration 4 — Broadcast notifications, QR modal, Onboarding checklist, and Voice-command toggle. 12/12 backend tests pass (100%): POST /api/rooms/{id}/broadcast returns {ok:true, sent:N} where N=host friend count, creates one room-invite notification per friend (verified via GET /api/notifications as each friend — type='room-invite', password, from_user_id, from_name, read=false match); wrong password → 401; non-participant → 403; no-friends host → sent=0. Regression for auth (register/login/me/forgot-password/reset-password/verify-email), rooms (create/join/active/history/promote/demote), rtc/ice — all green. Frontend e2e: fresh registered user sees OnboardingChecklist with heading 'Your first 5 scenes', all 5 step cards [photo,verify,friend,room,invite], progress bar at 0%; dismiss button hides it and persists `cinemasync.onboarding.dismissed.{userId}` in localStorage. CreateRoomForm renders [data-testid=create-room-notify-friends] checkbox (checked by default, label 'NOTIFY MY FRIENDS' for friendCount=0, ' · N' appears when host has friends — verified via backend friend-count=2 and toast 'Invite sent to 2 friend(s)' path in code). After submit, [data-testid=room-created-modal] appears with [data-testid=room-created-qr-canvas], room-created-qr-copy & room-created-qr-download buttons and 'Enter the lobby' button navigating to /lobby/{id}. Lobby has [data-testid=qr-panel] with password input + Generate-QR → [data-testid=lobby-qr-canvas] renders. WatchRoom has [data-testid=voice-command-toggle] visible for host; clicking (with microphone permission granted via context.grant_permissions) surfaces [data-testid=voice-listening-banner] with text 'Listening… say: play, pause, forward 30, back 10, reset'; second click removes banner (toggle state works).
critical_code_review_comments: QRCodeCard encodes plaintext password into the QR — intentional per spec, but worth noting the payload ends up in storage on devices scanning it.

RoomCreatedModal onClose navigates to lobby unconditionally (even if user clicks outside the modal or presses ✕). That matches the Enter-lobby flow since there's no explicit ✕, but clicking the dimmed overlay also triggers navigation — may surprise a user who wanted to stay on dashboard. Consider a tighter separation between Close and Enter actions.

broadcast endpoint happily re-broadcasts every time it is called — no de-dup on already-notified friends. A host re-creating rooms rapidly could spam the same friends. Consider collapsing or rate-limiting per (from_user_id, friend) pair.

OnboardingChecklist 'Send an invite' step uses `hasInvited = history.length > 0`, which is the same signal as 'host room'. So steps 4 and 5 complete simultaneously on first room create — the two steps effectively collapse into one. If intent was to track a genuine invite-sent event, derive from notifications where from_user_id=me and type=room-invite.
CORSMiddleware still uses allow_origins=['*'] + allow_credentials=True + allow_origin_regex='.*' (unchanged from iter 3).
server.py is now ~1300 lines with new endpoints — splitting into routers (auth, rooms, friends, admin, notifications) is overdue.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (checklist + notify checkbox + QR modal + lobby QR + voice toggle/banner all verified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Broadcast endpoint POST /api/rooms/{id}/broadcast {password} requires auth + participant + correct room password. Returns {ok:true, sent:N}. Inserts one db.notifications doc per friend (id, user_id=friend, type='room-invite', room_id, room_name, password (PLAINTEXT), from_user_id, from_name, read=false, created_at). Dashboard CreateRoomForm Notify-friends checkbox default=true; on submit calls /rooms then /rooms/{id}/broadcast only if notifyFriends && friendCount>0. Lobby qr-panel: password input + Generate button → renders lobby-qr-canvas. WatchRoom voice-command-toggle uses useVoiceCommands hook (Web Speech API); in headless testing, context.grant_permissions(['microphone']) is enough for the listening-banner state to toggle on click (the hook sets listening=true synchronously on start()). Actual speech recognition is NOT testable in headless. OnboardingChecklist keys localStorage as `cinemasync.onboarding.dismissed.{user.id}` — verified persistence. NOTE: steps 'room' and 'invite' share hasInvited=(history.length>0) so they complete in lock-step.
rca of the issue: n/a — all features behave as specified.
